puzzle.title: The Tale of the Sealed Well
puzzle.intro: A community received guidance but chose arrogance. Their story, etched in the annals of time, serves as a stark reminder of the consequences of rejecting truth. They silenced the voice of their warner, and in doing so, sealed their own fate. Can you uncover the identity of this lost community and the event that led to their ruin?
clues: We were a community among many who received a messenger to guide us.
Like the people of 'Ad and Thamud, we are mentioned in the Quran as a people who denied the truth.
Our story is a cautionary tale, a lesson for generations to come about the fate of those who disbelieve.
We lived by a source of water, a feature so central to our identity that it became part of our name.
We rejected our prophet, and for our disbelief, we faced a terrible punishment from Allah.
Our name, 'Ashab ar-Rass', literally translates to the 'Companions of the Well', forever linking our identity to our transgression.

trial.label: What was our primary sin?
options: Building a lofty tower to challenge God
Arrogantly boasting about a magnificent garden

outcome.label: What was our fate?
options: Guided to the straight path and forgiven
Saved by a believing insider from among them

puzzle.arabic: وَعَادًۭا وَثَمُودَا۟ وَأَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلرَّسِّ وَقُرُونًۢا بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ كَثِيرًۭا
puzzle.verse: And [We destroyed] ʿAad and Thamūd and the companions of the well and many generations between them. (25:38)
